svg.text: VIETNAMESE
ISOLATING GRAMMAR
VIETNAMESE WORD ORDER: SVO
Tôi ăn cơm. "I eat rice." (SVO like English)
Modifiers FOLLOW head: cơm trắng "rice white" = "white rice"
No inflection: no case, gender, number, tense on nouns/verbs
CLASSIFIERS
Nouns need classifiers when counted or with modifiers:
con = classifier for animals: con chó "CL dog" = "the dog"
cái = general classifier for things: cái bàn "CL table"
quả = classifier for round things: quả táo "CL apple"
TENSE THROUGH PARTICLES
No verb conjugation; particles mark time:
đã (past) • đang (progressive) • sẽ (future)
Tôi đã ăn cơm. "I already-ate rice." = "I ate rice."
PRONOUNS BASED ON KINSHIP
Vietnamese uses kin terms as pronouns:
tôi "I" (formal neutral) • anh "I/you" (older male) • chị "I/you" (older female)
em "I/you" (younger person) • bà "grandmother/ma'am"
ZHUANG GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ZHUANG WORD ORDER: SVO
Zhuang is also SVO (same as Vietnamese!)
Gou gwn haeux. "I eat rice." (identical SVO structure)
Modifiers follow head: same pattern as Vietnamese
ZHUANG CLASSIFIERS
Zhuang also uses classifiers! (Mainland SE Asia areal feature)
duz = classifier for animals • giz = classifier for people
Parallel classifier system to Vietnamese
SHARED MAINLAND SE ASIAN GRAMMAR
Both: SVO word order • both: isolating morphology
Both: classifiers • both: particles for tense/aspect
Both: modifiers AFTER noun head (not before as in English)
Both: no gender • both: no case inflection
These features: Mainland SE Asia Sprachbund convergence
PLATE 38c ALT · VIETNAMESE / ZHUANG · GRAMMAR
